subtitle.en: Run full containers right next to your Workers
subtitle.zh: 把完整的容器跑在 Workers 旁邊

tagline.en: When a Worker isn't enough, spin up a real container — your own Docker image, a full filesystem, any language — controlled from Worker code.
tagline.zh: 當 Worker 不夠用時，啟動一個真正的容器——你自己的 Docker 映像檔、完整的檔案系統、任何語言——而且全程由 Worker 程式碼控制。

text.en: Cloudflare Containers let you run a full container image — packaged with its own operating system, libraries, and files — on Cloudflare's network, started and managed directly from your Worker code.
text.zh: Cloudflare Containers 讓你在 Cloudflare 的網路上執行一個完整的容器映像檔（container image，把作業系統、函式庫和檔案全部打包在一起），而且由你的 Worker 程式碼直接啟動和管理。

text.en: A "container" is a self-contained box that holds an application plus everything it needs to run. The most common way to build one is with Docker. Containers are perfect when a lightweight Worker can't do the job — for example, when you need a specific programming language, lots of memory, multiple CPU cores, or a real disk.
text.zh: 「容器（container）」是一個自給自足的盒子，裡面裝著一個應用程式，以及它執行所需要的一切。最常見的打包方式是用 Docker。當輕量的 Worker 做不到某些事時，容器就派上用場——例如你需要某個特定的程式語言、大量記憶體、多核心 CPU、或是一顆真正的硬碟。

text.en: A Worker is like a quick errand you can do empty-handed. A container is like a fully packed suitcase — it carries everything your app needs (the OS, tools, files) so it runs the same way anywhere you set it down.
text.zh: Worker 像是兩手空空就能跑的快速差事；容器則像一個打包好的行李箱——它把你的 App 需要的一切（作業系統、工具、檔案）都裝在裡面，所以不管放到哪裡，跑起來都一模一樣。

text.en: Workers are fast and cheap, but they run in a sandbox with limited memory and only support a few languages. Some jobs simply need more. Containers fill that gap — without you having to learn Kubernetes or run your own servers.
text.zh: Workers 又快又便宜，但它跑在一個記憶體有限、只支援少數語言的沙盒裡。有些工作就是需要更多資源。Containers 正好補上這個缺口——而且你不用學 Kubernetes，也不用自己架伺服器。

text.en: Get up to 4 vCPUs and 12 GiB of memory — far beyond a Worker's 128 MB limit.
text.zh: 最多可用 4 個 vCPU 和 12 GiB 記憶體——遠超 Worker 的 128 MB 上限。

text.en: Run Python, Go, Java, FFmpeg, or any binary that runs on Linux — not just JavaScript.
text.zh: 可以跑 Python、Go、Java、FFmpeg，或任何能在 Linux 上執行的程式——不限於 JavaScript。

text.en: Read and write files on a full disk (up to 20 GB), great for processing or temporary storage.
text.zh: 在一顆完整的硬碟上讀寫檔案（最多 20 GB），很適合資料處理或暫存。

text.en: Already have a Docker image? Deploy it as-is — no rewrite needed.
text.zh: 已經有 Docker 映像檔了嗎？原封不動就能部署——不用改寫。

text.en: Cloudflare provisions, scales, and routes for you. No clusters, no orchestration to manage.
text.zh: Cloudflare 幫你佈署、擴充、導流。沒有叢集（cluster）、也沒有編排（orchestration）要管。

text.en: Containers are controlled from Worker code, so they plug straight into the rest of your stack.
text.zh: 容器由 Worker 程式碼控制，所以能直接接上你其他的服務。

text.en: Reach for Containers whenever a task is too heavy, too specialized, or too "Linux-y" for a plain Worker.
text.zh: 只要一項工作對單純的 Worker 來說太重、太特殊、或太「Linux」，就該考慮 Containers。

text.en: Transcode video or images with tools like FFmpeg that need CPU and a filesystem.
text.zh: 用 FFmpeg 之類需要 CPU 和檔案系統的工具，轉換影片或圖片格式。

text.en: Run data crunching, simulations, or model inference that exceeds Worker memory.
text.zh: 執行超出 Worker 記憶體的資料運算、模擬，或模型推論。

text.en: Lift an existing Python/Go/Java service into the Cloudflare network with its Docker image.
text.zh: 把現有的 Python / Go / Java 服務，連同 Docker 映像檔搬上 Cloudflare 網路。

text.en: Wrap a command-line tool that only ships as a Linux binary and call it from a Worker.
text.zh: 把只提供 Linux 執行檔的命令列工具包起來，再由 Worker 呼叫它。

text.en: Run user-supplied or AI-generated code in an isolated container sandbox.
text.zh: 在隔離的容器沙盒裡，執行使用者上傳或 AI 產生的程式碼。

text.en: Need a specific OS version, system library, or odd dependency? Bake it into the image.
text.zh: 需要特定的作業系統版本、系統函式庫或奇怪的相依套件？通通烤進映像檔裡。

text.en: You define a container with a Dockerfile, point your Worker at it in wrangler.jsonc, and deploy with Wrangler. Cloudflare builds the image and runs instances on demand. (Containers require the Workers Paid plan.)
text.zh: 你用一個 Dockerfile 定義容器，在 wrangler.jsonc 裡讓 Worker 指向它，再用 Wrangler 部署。Cloudflare 會建置映像檔，並依需求啟動執行個體。（Containers 需要 Workers 付費方案。）

text.en: Start from Cloudflare's official containers template, which includes a Dockerfile and Worker wired together.
text.zh: 從 Cloudflare 官方的 containers 範本開始，裡面已經把 Dockerfile 和 Worker 接好了。

text.en: In wrangler.jsonc, name your container class, point to your Dockerfile, and cap how many instances can run at once.
text.zh: 在 wrangler.jsonc 裡幫容器類別命名、指向你的 Dockerfile，並限制最多能同時跑幾個執行個體。

text.en: Wrangler builds your image, pushes it, and goes live. Instances start on demand and sleep when idle.
text.zh: Wrangler 會建置你的映像檔、推送上去並上線。執行個體會依需求啟動，閒置時自動休眠。

title.en: src/index.js — route a request to a container
Containers are built on Durable Objects
title.zh: 容器是建立在 Durable Objects 上的

text.en: Under the hood, each container is managed by a Durable Object — that's why the config has a durable_objects binding and a migration. You don't need to understand Durable Objects to use Containers, but it's why they integrate so smoothly.
text.zh: 在底層，每個容器都是由一個 Durable Object 管理的——這就是為什麼設定檔裡有 durable_objects 綁定和 migration。你不用懂 Durable Objects 也能用 Containers，但這正是它們整合得這麼順的原因。

text.en: The packaged blueprint of your app and its environment, usually built from a Dockerfile.
text.zh: 你 App 與其執行環境打包好的藍圖，通常由 Dockerfile 建置而來。

text.en: The size of the machine — from lite (256 MiB) up to standard-4 (4 vCPU, 12 GiB, 20 GB disk).
text.zh: 機器的大小——從 lite（256 MiB）一路到 standard-4（4 vCPU、12 GiB、20 GB 硬碟）。

text.en: Containers start when needed and sleep when idle, so you only pay while they're awake.
text.zh: 容器在需要時啟動、閒置時休眠，所以你只在它醒著時付費。

text.en: How long an idle container stays awake before sleeping to save resources, e.g. "10m".
text.zh: 閒置的容器在休眠省資源前，要先醒著多久，例如 "10m"（10 分鐘）。

text.en: A JavaScript class extending Container that defines your container's port and behavior.
text.zh: 一個繼承自 Container 的 JavaScript 類別，用來定義容器的連接埠和行為。

text.en: A cap on how many copies of your container can run simultaneously — useful for cost control.
text.zh: 限制同一時間最多能跑幾個容器副本——對控制成本很有用。

text.en: You're billed for memory and disk while a container is awake, and for vCPU only while it's actively computing. Set a sensible sleepAfter so idle containers don't keep running up the bill.
text.zh: 容器醒著時，你要為記憶體和硬碟付費；vCPU 則只在「實際運算」時計費。設定一個合理的 sleepAfter，閒置的容器才不會一直累積費用。

text.en: Instance types
text.zh: 執行個體規格

en: lite — 1/16 vCPU, 256 MiB memory, 2 GB disk
basic — 1/4 vCPU, 1 GiB memory, 4 GB disk
standard-1 — 1/2 vCPU, 4 GiB memory, 8 GB disk
standard-2 — 1 vCPU, 6 GiB memory, 12 GB disk
standard-3 — 2 vCPU, 8 GiB memory, 16 GB disk
standard-4 — 4 vCPU, 12 GiB memory, 20 GB disk
zh: lite — 1/16 vCPU、256 MiB 記憶體、2 GB 硬碟
basic — 1/4 vCPU、1 GiB 記憶體、4 GB 硬碟
standard-1 — 1/2 vCPU、4 GiB 記憶體、8 GB 硬碟
standard-2 — 1 vCPU、6 GiB 記憶體、12 GB 硬碟
standard-3 — 2 vCPU、8 GiB 記憶體、16 GB 硬碟
standard-4 — 4 vCPU、12 GiB 記憶體、20 GB 硬碟

text.en: Containers run on the $5/month Workers Paid plan, which includes monthly allowances (25 GiB-hours of memory, 375 vCPU-minutes, 200 GB-hours of disk) before usage-based charges kick in.
text.zh: Containers 需要每月 5 美元的 Workers 付費方案，方案內含每月免費額度（25 GiB-小時記憶體、375 vCPU-分鐘、200 GB-小時硬碟），超過才會開始按用量計費。
